title: Space Battle at Carida
content:

"Phantom" Multi-Role Stealth Starfighter/Legends|TIE/ph phantoms]] *1 [[HVs-2 hypervelocity gun]] |forces3= |forces4= |casual1=*Bossk leaves Consortium service *Moderate **At least 1 Interceptor IV frigate |casual2=All forces except the ''Admonitor'' and the ''Hound's Tooth'' |casual3= |casual4= |civilian= }} {{Quote|Tyber Zann, I'm come to collect your artifact for the Emperor. Comply with my demands, and I promise a swift and reasonably honorable death.''"
"''Thrawn, of course. I knew you were still lurking in my shadows. Don't tell me you're the contact Xizor arranged.''"
"''In a matter of speaking, yes. Xizor is dead.|Thrawn and Tyber Zann, just before the battle|}} The '''Space Battle at Carida''' took place during the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]], above the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Carida/Legends|Carida]], circa [[3 ABY/Legends|3 ABY]]–[[4 ABY/Legends|4 ABY]]. The forces of the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]] under the command of [[Grand Admiral/Legends|Grand Admiral]] [[Mitth'raw'nuruodo/Legends|Thrawn]] aboard his [[Flagship/Legends|flagship]], the ''[[Admonitor]]'', entered into [[Space warfare/Legends|combat]] with the [[Zann Consortium/Legends|Zann Consortium]], led by [[Crime lord/Legends|crime lord]] [[Tyber Zann]] in his new [[Starship/Legends|flagship]], the [[Merciless (Aggressor-class)|''Merciless'']], over a [[Carbonite Sith Army holocron|Sith holocron]] that Zann wanted to sell to the Empire. Once he arrived Thrawn demanded that he hand over the holocron. Zann refused and the [[Fleet/Legends|fleets]] engaged each other and suffered damage until [[Bossk/Legends|Bossk]], a [[Bounty hunter/Legends|bounty hunter]] who worked for Zann, stole the [[Sith holocron/Legends|holocron]] and brought it to Thrawn, who fled afterward. After Thrawn fled the Imperial fleet was destroyed without his guidance and the Consortium fleet emerged victorious. Zann, who had anticipated Bossk's betrayal, then used a [[Homing beacon/Legends|homing beacon]] on the [[Holocron/Legends|holocron]] to lead him back to the device. ==Prelude== {{Quote|Xizor takes the fall, we get the gas and our imperial contact. An excellent plan.|Urai Fen|}} Determined to sell a [[Carbonite Sith Army holocron|Sith holocron]] to the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]], which he [[Battle of Felucia (Zann Consortium)|had stolen]] from [[Jabba Desilijic Tiure/Legends|Jabba]] the [[Hutt/Legends|Hutt]], [[Crime lord/Legends|crime lord]] [[Tyber Zann]] agreed to steal [[Tibanna/Legends|Tibanna gas]] from [[Bespin/Legends|Bespin]] for [[Black Sun/Legends|Black Sun]] leader{{DB|character|princexizor|Xizor, Prince}} [[Xizor/Legends|Xizor]] in exchange for a meeting with the Empire and half the gas. During the [[Ground Battle on Bespin|raid]], Zann, along with his old friend [[Urai Fen]] and the [[Droid/Legends|droid]] [[Bounty hunter/Legends|bounty hunter]]{{DB|droid|ig88|IG-88}} [[IG-88A/Legends|IG-88A]], planted evidence to frame Xizor and his organization with intentions of keeping all the gas for himself. Unaware that he had been framed, Xizor set up the meeting. However, Imperial [[Supreme Commander]] [[Anakin Skywalker/Legends|Darth Vader]] was convinced that Black Sun was responsible for the theft and had Xizor [[Battle over Coruscant|killed]]. ==The battle== {{Quote|Bossk has stolen the artifact. He's heading toward Thrawn's ship! All fire on the ''Hound's Tooth''!''"
"''No! Let him go! Let him go!"|A Consortium soldier and Tyber Zann during the battle|}} [[File:Merciless_Aggressor.jpg|thumb|left|200px|Tyber Zann's new flagship, the ''Merciless'', made its debut during the Space Battle at Carida.]] When Zann's [[Interceptor IV-class frigate|Interceptor IV frigate]] left [[Hyperspace/Legends|hyperspace]] at [[Carida/Legends|Carida]], it was met by [[Grand Admiral/Legends|Grand Admiral]] [[Mitth'raw'nuruodo/Legends|Thrawn's]] [[Flagship/Legends|flagship]], the ''[[Admonitor]]'', which led an Imperial armada of [[Imperial I-class Star Destroyer/Legends|''Imperial I''-class Star Destroyers]], [[Victory I-class Star Destroyer/Legends|''Victory I''-class Star Destroyers]], [[Tartan-class patrol cruiser|''Tartan''-class patrol cruisers]], [[TIE/d "Defender" Multi-Role Starfighter/Legends|TIE/D Defenders]], [[TIE/sa tactical bomber/Legends|TIE/sa bombers]] and [[TIE/IN interceptor/Legends|TIE/IN interceptors]]. Thrawn then double-crossed Zann by informing him of Xizor's [[Death/Legends|death]] and demanding the artifact be turned over in exchange for a quick and somewhat honorable death. Zann responded by bringing in his [[Fleet/Legends|fleet]] of [[StarViper-class attack platform/Legends|''StarViper''-class attack platforms]], [[GAT-12 Skipray Blastboat]]s, [[Crusader-class corvette|''Crusader''-class corvettes]], Interceptor IV [[Frigate/Legends|frigates]] and [[Vengeance-class frigate|''Vengeance''-class frigates]], which he had stationed nearby. Thrawn, in turn, countered by firing a hidden [[HVs-2 hypervelocity gun]] on the [[Planet/Legends|planet's]] surface, which destroyed an Interceptor IV frigate. Zann brought his new flagship, the [[Aggressor-class destroyer|''Aggressor''-class Star Destroyer]] [[Merciless (Aggressor-class)|''Merciless'']], out of hyperspace. The ship destroyed a ''Victory I''-class Star Destroyer with its massive guns. The new weapon surprised Thrawn, and the two fleets [[Space warfare/Legends|engaged]] each other. After intense combat between the two fleets, Zann was apparently betrayed by his henchman [[Bossk/Legends|Bossk]], who stole the Sith artifact and absconded to his ship, the [[Hound's Tooth/Legends|''Hound's Tooth'']], to take it to the ''Admonitor''. A Consortium officer ordered the fleet to fire on Bossk, but Zann belayed the order, telling them to let him go. Bossk then delivered it into the hands of Thrawn. The outnumbered Grand Admiral retreated in the severely damaged ''Admonitor'' with his prize albeit secured, and left his fleet to deal with Zann's forces however it was later destroyed. ==Aftermath== {{Quote|Goodbye Tyber, for the last time.|Thrawn|}} Bossk left the Consortium's employment after he received a huge amount of [[Galactic Credit Standard/Legends|credits]] from the Empire. Zann had predicted Bossk's betrayal and had attached a [[Homing beacon/Legends|homing beacon]] to the artifact which eventually led him to the [[Imperial Archives (museum)/Legends|Imperial Archives]] on [[Coruscant/Legends|Coruscant]]. This set the stage for the [[Raid on the Imperial Archive|Theft on Coruscant]] in which Zann planed to steal access codes for the [[Eclipse (Eclipse-class)|''Eclipse'']], [[Emperor/Legends|Emperor]] [[Palpatine/Legends|Palpatine's]] new flagship under construction over [[Kuat/Legends|Kuat]], so he could gain access to the Emperor's store houses, containing the wealth Zann needed to expand his criminal organization. The ''Admonitor'' was repaired, and Thrawn returned to the employ of the Empire. The successful performance of the ''Aggressor''-class Star Destroyer saw more of them put into full production, and Zann Consortium [[Shipyard/Legends|shipyards]] churned them out for future battles. Many later participated in the [[Battle for the Eclipse|Battle for the ''Eclipse'']] between the Consortium, the Empire and the Rebellion over the ''Eclipse''. ==Behind the scenes== The space battle at Carida first appeared in the [[2006]] [[Star Wars video games|video game]] ''[[Star Wars: Empire at War: Forces of Corruption]]'' for the [[Wikipedia:Personal computer|PC]]. Its name was identified in the [[Star Wars: Empire at War: Forces of Corruption Expansion: Prima Official Game Guide|Prima Guide]] for the game. In the game, the Zann Consortium fleet composition is decided by the player; the Imperial fleet first appears in a cutscene and its composition is attributed to game mechanics. After Thrawn gets the holocron and flees, the player must destroy the rest of the Imperial fleet. After the battle, Tyber Zann is onboard the ''Merciless'' in all space battles. The ''Aggressor''-class Star Destroyer also becomes available for production to the Zann Consortium. Bossk is also no longer available as a hero unit in the game's story mode from that point on, meaning the Fondor piracy mission cannot be done. The ''Forces of Corruption'' Prima Guide offers several tips for wining the battle, and advises that before the battle, the player sends a [[defiler]]–a class of Consortium [[Infantry/Legends|infantry]]–to corrupt Carida for Tyber Zann. It also advises that the player send to Carida a fleet consisting of seven ''Vengeance''-class frigates, four or five Interceptor IV frigates, and six ''Crusader''-class corvettes, with the rest of the armada consisting of ''StarViper''-class attack platforms and GAT-12 Skipray Blastboats. The guide recommends that at the start of the battle, the player sends all of their [[Capital ship/Legends|capital ships]] and [[Bomber/Legends|bombers]] to attack the ''Admonitor'', whilst sending their [[Starfighter/Legends|starfighters]] to attack the Imperial fighters and bombers, and also deploying the ''StarViper''s' [[Pistoeka sabotage droid/Legends|buzz droids]]. It also states that if the Imperial [[Star Destroyer/Legends|Star Destroyers]] try to capture the player's ships with their [[Tractor beam/Legends|tractor beams]], the player should order their ships to target the Star Destroyers' tractor beams to free the Consortium ships from their grasp. After Thrawn flees the battle, the guide suggests that the player should direct the Consortium forces to target the Imperial ships that have the most weapons and destroy their [[Laser cannon/Legends|laser cannons]], then change target and destroy the laser cannons on the other Star Destroyers. Once all of the Imperial ships' weapons are offline, the guide directs the player to finish off the weakened Star Destroyers. If the mission does not end when all of the Imperial capital ships have been destroyed, the guide advises the player to check a nearby [[Nebula/Legends|nebula]] for any ships that may be hiding there.[[Star Wars: Empire at War: Forces of Corruption Expansion: Prima Official Game Guide|''Star Wars: Empire at War: Forces of Corruption Expansion'': Prima Official Game Guide]] ==Appearances== *''[[Star Wars: Empire at War: Forces of Corruption]]'' {{1st}} ==Sources== *[[Star Wars: Empire at War: Forces of Corruption Expansion: Prima Official Game Guide|''Star Wars: Empire at War: Forces of Corruption Expansion'': Prima Official Game Guide]] {{1stID|Space Battle at Carida}} ==Notes and references== {{Reflist}} {{Interlang |de=Schlacht von Carida (3 NSY) }} {{DEFAULTSORT:Carida, Space Battle at}} [[Category:Battles of Carida]] [[Category:Events of the Galactic Civil War]]
